⭐ Health and Physical Education

Diseases, Smoking and Alcoholism

Learn how diseases spread and how to prevent them!

📚 Unit 4 ⏱️ 7 min read

Unit 4: Diseases, Smoking and Alcoholism

Diseases make our body unhealthy. Some diseases can spread from one person to another. We must know how to prevent them.

Lesson 1: Communicable Diseases

What is a Disease?

A disease is a condition that makes the body function poorly. It causes symptoms like fever, pain, cough, or weakness.

Types of Diseases

TypeMeaningCan it spread?Examples
CommunicableSpreads from person to personYesCold, flu, COVID-19, chickenpox
Non-communicableDoes not spreadNoDiabetes, asthma, cancer

What are Communicable Diseases?

Communicable diseases (also called infectious diseases) are illnesses caused by germs (microorganisms) that spread from one person to another.

How Do Germs Spread?

Mode of TransmissionHow it spreadsExamples
Through airCoughing, sneezing, talkingCold, flu, COVID-19, tuberculosis
Through waterDrinking contaminated waterCholera, typhoid, diarrhea
Through foodEating contaminated foodFood poisoning
Through touchTouching an infected personSkin infections, scabies
Through insectsMosquitoes, flies, fleasMalaria, dengue, plague
Through bloodSharing needles, blood transfusionHIV/AIDS, hepatitis B

Common Communicable Diseases

DiseaseCaused byMode of spreadSymptomsPrevention
Common coldVirusAir (cough/sneeze)Runny nose, sneezing, coughCover mouth, wash hands
Flu (Influenza)VirusAirFever, body ache, coughFlu vaccine
COVID-19CoronavirusAirFever, cough, loss of tasteMask, distancing, vaccine
ChickenpoxVirusAir, touchRed blisters, feverVaccine
MeaslesVirusAirRed rash, high feverVaccine (MMR)
DiarrheaBacteria/virusWater, foodLoose stools, dehydrationDrink clean water, wash hands
CholeraBacteriaWaterSevere diarrhea, vomitingClean drinking water
TyphoidBacteriaFood, waterHigh fever, stomach painTyphoid vaccine
Tuberculosis (TB)BacteriaAir (cough)Cough for weeks, weight lossBCG vaccine
MalariaParasiteMosquito biteFever, chills, headacheMosquito nets, repellent
DengueVirusMosquito biteHigh fever, joint painPrevent mosquito breeding
Hepatitis BVirusBlood, body fluidsYellow skin (jaundice)Hepatitis B vaccine
HIV/AIDSVirusBlood, unprotected sexWeak immune systemSafe practices

How to Prevent Communicable Diseases

Prevention methodHow to do it
Wash handsWash with soap and water before eating and after toilet
Cover mouthUse elbow or tissue when coughing/sneezing
Get vaccinatedTake all recommended vaccines
Drink clean waterBoil or filter water before drinking
Eat clean foodWash fruits and vegetables before eating
Keep distanceStay away from sick people
Wear a maskIn crowded or sick areas
Clean surroundingsNo standing water for mosquitoes
Do not share personal itemsTowels, combs, razors, water bottles
Stay home when sickDo not go to school if you are sick

The Importance of Handwashing

When to wash hands:

  • Before eating
  • After using the toilet
  • After coughing or sneezing
  • After playing outside
  • After touching animals
  • Before touching your face

Proper handwashing steps:

  1. Wet hands with clean water
  2. Apply soap
  3. Rub palms, back of hands, between fingers, under nails (20 seconds)
  4. Rinse well
  5. Dry with a clean towel

💡 Sing the “Happy Birthday” song twice – that is 20 seconds!

Vaccination (Immunization)

Vaccination is giving a medicine (vaccine) that protects you from a disease.

VaccineProtects against
BCGTuberculosis (TB)
DPTDiphtheria, Pertussis, Tetanus
PolioPoliomyelitis
MMRMeasles, Mumps, Rubella
Hepatitis BHepatitis B
TyphoidTyphoid
COVID-19Coronavirus

💡 Vaccines are safe and save millions of lives every year!

Smoking and Alcoholism (Brief Overview)

Note: These topics are introduced at Class 5 level to create awareness.

Smoking

FactExplanation
What is it?Inhaling smoke from burning tobacco (cigarettes, bidi)
Harmful substancesNicotine, tar, carbon monoxide (poisonous)
Health effectsLung cancer, breathing problems, heart disease, yellow teeth

Effects of Smoking on the Body

Body partEffect
LungsDamage, cancer, coughing
HeartBlocked arteries
MouthYellow teeth, bad breath
SkinEarly wrinkles, dull skin

⚠️ Secondhand smoke (smoke from someone else’s cigarette) is also harmful to people nearby.

Alcoholism

FactExplanation
What is it?Drinking too much alcohol regularly
Health effectsLiver damage, memory loss, poor judgment
Social effectsFamily problems, accidents, poor performance in school/work

Why Children Should Avoid Smoking and Alcohol

ReasonExplanation
Harms growing bodyBody is still developing
Affects brainPoor memory and concentration
Wastes moneyExpensive habit
Bad exampleInfluences younger children
AddictionHard to quit once started

💡 Just say NO to smoking and alcohol!

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Fill in the Blanks

  1. Diseases that spread from person to person are called ______ diseases.
  2. Common cold spreads through the ______.
  3. ______ is a disease spread by mosquito bites.
  4. Washing hands with ______ kills germs.
  5. A ______ protects us from a disease.

Exercise 2: True or False

  1. All diseases are communicable. (T/F)
  2. Vaccines prevent communicable diseases. (T/F)
  3. Mosquitoes spread cholera. (T/F)
  4. We should share towels with friends. (T/F)
  5. Smoking damages the lungs. (T/F)

Exercise 3: Match the Disease to Its Mode of Spread

DiseaseMode of spread
Colda) Water
Cholerab) Air (cough/sneeze)
Malariac) Air (cough)
Tuberculosisd) Mosquito bite

Exercise 4: Write Two Prevention Methods

For each disease, write two ways to prevent it.

  1. Common cold: ____________ and ____________
  2. Cholera: ____________ and ____________
  3. Malaria: ____________ and ____________

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. communicable, 2. air, 3. Malaria/Dengue, 4. soap, 5. vaccine

Exercise 2: 1. F, 2. T, 3. F, 4. F, 5. T

Exercise 3: Cold→b, Cholera→a, Malaria→d, Tuberculosis→c

Great work! Prevention is better than cure. Protect yourself and others from diseases!
